content.content: 79:  --color-bg-muted: var(--bg-muted);

1→# Code Reviewer Memory
     2→
     3→## Key Architecture Patterns (Confirmed)
     4→
     5→- `react-resizable-panels` uses percentage values for `collapsedSize`/`minSize` — pixel↔percent
     6→  conversion via `panelGroupContainerRef` (excludes agent-server strip from container width math)
     7→- `workspaceLayoutStore` uses `version: 9` migrations (v9 added terminal tab state) — increment version when adding persisted fields
     8→- `cn()` uses `twMerge` internally — arbitrary `animate-[...]` classes conflict-resolve correctly
     9→  (last wins), so conditional breathing override pattern with `cn()` works as intended
    10→- `data-slot="resizable-panel"` is the CSS hook for the flex-grow transition in global.css
    11→
    12→## Animation Patterns
    13→
    14→- `strip-breathe` + `strip-settle` keyframes live in `global.css` (multi-component reuse)
    15→- Single-component animations should use Framer Motion inline, NOT global.css keyframes
    16→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
    17→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` + `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` use
    18→  `motion` (pre-existing violation).
    19→
    20→## Dead Code Pattern (Confirmed)
    21→
    22→- When removing a prop from a call site, also check the destructuring site of the hook.
    23→  TypeScript does not error on unused destructured variables — only ESLint's `no-unused-vars` catches this.
    24→
    25→## CSS Architecture
    26→
    27→- `transition: flex-grow` on `[data-slot="resizable-panel"]` — flex-grow IS animatable per CSS spec
    28→- `window-resizing` class disables ALL layout transitions during native window resize
    29→- `[data-resize-handle-active]` sibling/parent selector disables panel transitions during drag
    30→
    31→## Cross-Component Event Bus Pattern
    32→
    33→- `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("insert-to-chat", { detail }))` is the established
    34→  pattern for browser panel → chat input communication.
    35→
    36→## XML Attribute Serialization Risk Pattern
    37→
    38→- `serializeInspectElement` in `parseInspectTags.ts` embeds user-controlled strings (innerText,
    39→  path, tagName, reactComponent) into XML attributes with NO escaping. A `"` breaks parsing.
    40→  Fix: HTML-escape values before embedding.
    41→
    42→## Monorepo Source Path Map (CRITICAL — always verify before writing path globs)
    43→
    44→- `shared/schema.ts` — DB schema, indexes, triggers (single source of truth, NOT in backend/lib/)
    45→- `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts` — DB connection + initialization
    46→- `apps/backend/src/routes/**`, `apps/backend/src/services/**`, `apps/backend/src/middleware/**`
    47→- `apps/agent-server/index.ts`, `apps/agent-server/rpc-connection.ts`, `apps/agent-server/protocol.ts`
    48→- `apps/agent-server/agents/**`, `apps/agent-server/event-broadcaster.ts`
    49→- `apps/desktop/main/index.ts` — Electron app init + lifecycle
    50→- `apps/desktop/main/backend-process.ts` — backend process management
    51→- `apps/desktop/main/native-handlers.ts` — Electron IPC handlers (replaces Tauri commands)
    52→- `apps/desktop/main/browser-views.ts` — BrowserView management
    53→- `apps/web/src/platform/**` — platform abstraction (IPC, WebSocket)
    54→- `apps/web/src/features/*/api/**`, `apps/web/src/features/*/ui/**`, `apps/web/src/features/*/store/**`
    55→- `apps/web/src/shared/**`, `apps/web/src/global.css`, `apps/web/src/app/**`
    56→- `apps/web/src/components/ui/**` — Shadcn base components
    57→- `src-tauri/` DOES NOT EXIST — the project migrated from Tauri (Rust) to Electron. No Cargo.toml, no .rs files.
    58→- `agent-server/db/` DOES NOT EXIST — agent-server is stateless (no DB). Schema lives in `shared/schema.ts`.
    59→
    60→## Distribution / CI Patterns
    61→
    62→- `sed -i ''` is macOS/BSD syntax — fails on ubuntu-latest (GNU sed needs `sed -i "..."`).
    63→- The app is Electron (not Tauri). No Cargo.toml, no `cargo test`, no entitlements.plist at `src-tauri/`.
    64→- The app spawns system `node` binary for backend/agent-server child processes.
    65→
    66→## Sentry / Observability Patterns (Confirmed)
    67→
    68→- **DSN propagation**: Rust `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_RUST")` bakes frontend DSN at compile time.
    69→  `option_env!("SENTRY_DSN_NODE")` is baked into the binary and forwarded as `SENTRY_DSN` env var
    70→  to child processes (backend + agent-server). Frontend uses `import.meta.env.VITE_SENTRY_DSN`.
    71→- **`option_env!` with empty string**: When `SENTRY_DSN_RUST` is unset, `option_env!` returns `None`,
    72→  and `.unwrap_or("")` passes `""` to `sentry::init`. The sentry-rust crate treats empty DSN as
    73→  disabled — no panic, no-op client. This pattern is correct and safe.
    74→- **Conditional init in Node.js**: `if (process.env.SENTRY_DSN) { Sentry.init(...) }` means when
    75→  DSN is absent, Sentry is never initialized. `Sentry.captureException()` and `Sentry.close()` called
    76→  on an uninitialized client are safe no-ops in `@sentry/node@10`. Correct pattern.
    77→- **Frontend `enabled: !import.meta.env.DEV`**: Sentry is initialized but disabled in dev mode.
    78→  `captureException()` calls in `errorReporting.ts` are no-ops in dev mode as a result. Good behavior.
    79→- **`sendDefaultPii: true`** is set on all layers — intentional (desktop app, known users).
    80→- **Sentry guard lifetime (Rust)**: `_sentry_guard` in `main()` lives until process exit. Dropping
    81→  it earlier would flush pending events prematurely. The single-underscore prefix suppresses the
    82→  unused-variable warning without dropping it.
    83→- **`Sentry.close(2000)` before `process.exit`**: The 2-second flush window is appropriate. Both
    84→  backend and agent-server implement this correctly in their `uncaughtException` handlers.
    85→- **Hardcoded Sentry org/project in vite.config.ts**: `org: "deus-40"` and
    86→  `project: "deus-desktop-frontend"` are hardcoded. These are metadata identifiers (not secrets).
    87→  Leaking them poses minimal risk but couples the open-source repo to internal Sentry project names.
    88→- **CI missing Sentry secrets**: `release.yml` does not pass `SENTRY_DSN_RUST`, `SENTRY_DSN_NODE`,
    89→  `VITE_SENTRY_DSN`, or `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`. Production builds will have Sentry monitoring disabled
    90→  until these are wired into the workflow.
    91→- **Root ErrorBoundary not connected to Sentry**: The outer `<ConditionalErrorBoundary fallback={DashboardError}>`
    92→  at line 126 of App.tsx has NO `onError` prop. React crashes inside MainLayout that are caught by
    93→  that boundary go unreported to Sentry. Only the inner boundary (line 187) calls `reportError`.
    94→- **`@sentry/node` in production dependencies**: `@sentry/node` and `@sentry/vite-plugin` are in
    95→  `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`). `@sentry/vite-plugin` is build-only and should be in
    96→  `devDependencies`. `@sentry/node` is needed at runtime by backend/agent-server, correct in `dependencies`.
    97→
    98→## Experimental Feature Toggle Pattern (Confirmed)
    99→
   100→- `experimental_*` fields are `boolean | undefined` on `Settings` — `undefined` means ON (backwards-compat)
   101→- `isTabVisible(tab, settings)` is exported from `ContentTabBar.tsx`
   102→- `effectiveContentTab` in `MainContent` = `isTabVisible(raw, settings) ? raw : "changes"` (was "code" before Changes rename)
   103→- BUG (unfixed): `isTabVisible` returns `settings?.[key] === true`, which returns `false` while settings is loading.
   104→  Correct: treat `undefined` as `true` for experimental tabs (`val === undefined || val === true`).
   105→
   106→## workspaceLayoutStore Setter Inconsistency (Confirmed Bug)
   107→
   108→- `setLayout` does a three-way merge: `{ ...defaultLayout, ...existingLayout, ...updates }` — correct.
   109→- All individual setters (`setActiveContentTab`, `setSelectedFilePath`, etc.) do only a two-way merge:
   110→  `{ ...(state.layouts[id] || defaultLayout), field: value }`. When a record exists but lacks a new field
   111→  (e.g. after a version bump), the individual setters will NOT backfill from `defaultLayout`.
   112→  Fix: use the same three-way pattern in all individual setters.
   113→- Store is now at version 10 (bumped during Files-as-top-level-tab refactor). `migrate` wipes all layouts.
   114→
   115→## Simulator / Electron IPC Command Patterns (Confirmed)
   116→
   117→- Simulator IPC handlers live in `apps/desktop/main/native-handlers.ts` (Electron, not Tauri/Rust).
   118→- Three-state probe pattern: `null | true | false`. `null` = loading, suppresses button flicker.
   119→
   120→## Simulator State Machine Patterns (Confirmed)
   121→
   122→- `dispatch()` = validated transition; `setSession()` = recovery bypass for external observation.
   123→- `handleStop()` dispatches STOP first (optimistic), then awaits IPC — avoids UI stuck in "active".
   124→- `STREAM_READY` requires `current.udid === event.udid` — prevents start/stop race.
   125→
   126→## Framer Motion Patterns (Confirmed)
   127→
   128→- `LazyMotion` with `domAnimation` wraps the whole app in `ThemeProvider.tsx` — always use `m`
   129→  (compact alias) instead of `motion`. Using `motion` bypasses lazy loading and pulls in the full
   130→  bundle. Files `AgentQuestionOverlay.tsx` and `PlanApprovalOverlay.tsx` currently use `motion`
   131→  (not `m`) — this is a pre-existing violation, not introduced by new code.
   132→- `useReducedMotion()` is the established pattern for honoring prefers-reduced-motion. Used in
   133→  `RepositoryItem`, `ToolUseBlock`, `ThinkingBlock`, `PastedImageCard`, `PastedTextCard`,
   134→  `CloneRepositoryModal`. New animated components should follow the same pattern.
   135→- `AnimatePresence` exit animation bug: if a component conditionally returns `null` before
   136→  `AnimatePresence` renders, the exit animation never fires. Pattern must be:
   137→  `<AnimatePresence>{condition && <m.div exit={...}>...</m.div>}</AnimatePresence>` — NOT
   138→  `{condition ? <AnimatePresence><m.div>...</m.div></AnimatePresence> : null}`.
   139→- `AnimatePresence` inside `.map()` anti-pattern: placing `<AnimatePresence>` as the return value
   140→  of a `.map()` call (one per item) means the entire boundary is recreated when the item's key
   141→  changes. When the condition flips (e.g., `isEditing` changes from item A to item B), the boundary
   142→  for A is torn down in the same commit as its child — `AnimatePresence` never sees the child
   143→  transition and the exit animation does not fire. Fix: single `<AnimatePresence>` outside the
   144→  `.map()`, with stable keys on each animated child element.
   145→
   146→## Global queryClient Defaults (Confirmed)
   147→
   148→- `refetchOnWindowFocus: false` globally in `queryClient.ts` — critical to prevent typing lag.
   149→  `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` only acceptable for git diff queries (intentional).
   150→
   151→## CORS / External Fetch Patterns
   152→
   153→- Statuspage.io APIs include `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` — works from WKWebView.
   154→  Third-party dependency: if headers are removed, fetches silently fail with CORS error.
   155→
   156→## Design Token Completeness
   157→
   158→- `bg-accent-green`, `bg-accent-gold`, `bg-accent-red` ARE valid — defined in `@theme`.
   159→- `bg-warning`, `bg-success`, `text-warning` ARE valid — `--color-warning`/`--color-success` in `@theme`.
   160→
   161→## Analytics (PostHog) Patterns
   162→
   163→- Full details in `analytics-patterns.md`
   164→- Consent model: OPT-OUT (`analytics_enabled !== false` = default ON). Comment in `settings.ts` fixed.
   165→- `_enabled` in `track.ts` defaults to `false` (consent-first). No pre-consent tracking window.
   166→- `app_launched` guarded by `appLaunchTracked` ref — fires once per app lifecycle.
   167→- `onboarding_started` guarded by `onboardingTrackedRef` — StrictMode-safe.
   168→- Analytics toggle in GeneralSection.tsx awaits `saveSetting` and reverts on failure.
   169→- `VITE_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_KEY` in `.env.example` is safe to commit — PostHog ingestion keys are write-only.
   170→- `posthog-js/react` is a subpath export of `posthog-js` — no separate dep needed.
   171→
   172→## Content Panel Redesign Pattern (Confirmed)
   173→
   174→- "Code" tab renamed to "Changes". "Files" promoted from sub-tab inside Code to a top-level tab.
   175→- `RightSidePanel` monolith replaced by thin `ContentView` router (`app/layouts/ContentView.tsx`).
   176→- `ChangesView` (`features/workspace/ui/ChangesView.tsx`) is self-contained: fetches its own
   177→  `useFileChanges`, `useUncommittedFiles`, `useLastTurnFiles` data — parent only provides `workspace`.
   178→- `AllFilesDiffViewer`: `hideHeader?: boolean` prop (default false) for embedding in ChangesView.
   179→- Changes layout: AllFilesDiffViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | DiffFilesTree (25%).
   180→- Files layout: FileViewer (75%) | ResizableHandle | FileBrowserPanel (25%).
   181→- `PersistentTab` (`app/layouts/PersistentTab.tsx`): thin wrapper for always-mounted tabs using
   182→  `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` CSS-hide pattern.
   183→
   184→## Circular Self-Import Pattern (Recurring Risk)
   185→
   186→- `ChangesView.tsx` imports `useFileChanges` et al. from `"@/features/workspace"` — the barrel that
   187→  re-exports `ChangesView` itself. Bundler resolves it, but it is fragile for HMR.
   188→  Fix: import hooks directly from their source (`../api/workspace.queries`, `../hooks/useWorkspaceLayout`).
   189→- Watch for this pattern whenever a component in `features/X/ui/` is also exported from `features/X/index.ts`
   190→  and then imports from `"@/features/X"`.
   191→
   192→## ContentView Data Ownership Pattern
   193→
   194→- `ContentView` is a routing shell — data fetching belongs in leaf tab components, not in the router.
   195→- The `useFileChanges` call inside `ContentView` (for the Files tab) is a leak of this pattern.
   196→  TanStack Query deduplicates the network call, so it is not a perf issue — but ownership is wrong.
   197→  Fix: move into the Files tab branch or into `FileBrowserPanel` itself.
   198→
   199→## Always-Mount CSS Hide Pattern (Confirmed)
   200→
   201→- Browser, Terminal, and Simulator panels use `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` (not `hidden`)
   202→  when inactive — keeps DOM alive so PTY/WebSocket sessions survive tab switches.
   203→- Pattern: `<div className={cn("h-full w-full", activeTab !== "terminal" && "pointer-events-none invisible absolute")}>`
   204→- `offsetParent === null` check in ResizeObserver guards against `fitAddon.fit()` being called when
   205→  terminal container is CSS-hidden (`invisible` sets `visibility: hidden`, which makes `offsetParent` null for
   206→  absolutely-positioned elements but NOT for `invisible` alone — `invisible absolute` together does make
   207→  `offsetParent` null in most cases, but it is fragile; the `offsetWidth/offsetHeight === 0` check is more reliable).
   208→- `partialize` in the layout store strips terminal tab state (tabs/activeId/nextNum) — PTY processes
   209→  don't survive app restarts; zombie tab metadata would reference dead processes.
   210→- The `updateTabs` helper in TerminalPanel captures `nextTerminalNum` from the _render_ snapshot —
   211→  stale-closure risk exists in the `pendingTask`/`pendingCommand` useEffect callbacks because
   212→  `tabs` and `nextTerminalNum` are excluded from their deps arrays (suppressed with eslint-disable).
   213→
   214→## Multi-Workspace Terminal Persistence Pattern (TerminalPanel.tsx)
   215→
   216→- `visitedWorkspaces: Map<workspaceId, workspacePath>` in TerminalPanel state keeps one
   217→  `WorkspaceTerminals` subtree mounted per visited workspace — PTYs survive workspace switches.
   218→- Non-current workspace terminals use same `pointer-events-none invisible absolute h-full w-full`
   219→  CSS-hide pattern inside a `relative` container.
   220→- `WorkspaceTerminals` sub-component uses per-workspace Zustand selectors for isolation.
   221→- Known leak: `visitedWorkspaces` has no eviction path when a workspace is deleted. PTYs for
   222→  deleted workspaces stay alive until TerminalPanel unmounts. Needs workspace deletion event hook.
   223→- `pendingTask` useEffect risk: if task queued just before workspace switch, the tab opens in the
   224→  NEW workspace because `workspaceId` in deps reflects the current workspace at effect fire time.
   225→- Terminal.tsx `initialCommand` is in the main effect dep array but only consumed once at mount.
   226→  Safe in practice because `id` is globally unique (`crypto.randomUUID()`), so effect runs once per terminal.
   227→  Cleaner to exclude `initialCommand` from deps and snapshot it inside the effect.
   228→
   229→## AgentHandler Interface Patterns (Confirmed)
   230→
   231→- Renamed methods: `handleQuery→query`, `handleCancel→cancel`, `handleReset→reset` (completed in #179)
   232→- `AgentCapabilities` struct added to `AgentHandler` interface — all 4 booleans required
   233→- Optional Claude-specific methods on `AgentHandler`: `auth?`, `initWorkspace?`, `getContextUsage?`,
   234→  `updatePermissionMode?` — guarded by optional-chaining at dispatch in `index.ts`
   235→- `ContextUsageParams` in `agent-handler.ts` has shape `{id, options: {cwd, claudeSessionId}}`.
   236→  The call site in `index.ts` passes `request` (type `Omit<ContextUsageRequest, "type">`) which has an
   237→  extra `agentType` field — TypeScript accepts this as structural subtyping, but it means
   238→  `ContextUsageParams` is effectively a subset alias of `ContextUsageRequest`.
   239→- `capabilities` field is declared but never read at dispatch sites — all dispatch guards use optional
   240→  chaining on the method itself (`agent?.auth`), not `agent.capabilities.auth`. The `capabilities`
   241→  object currently serves documentation purposes only (no runtime enforcement).
   242→- `updatePermissionMode` dispatch in `index.ts` silently no-ops when method is absent (no rejection),
   243→  while `auth` and `initWorkspace` reject with an error. Intentionally asymmetric: permission mode
   244→  is fire-and-forget; auth/workspaceInit are request-response.
   245→- Old method name stale references remain in: `claude-session.ts` JSDoc comments (lines 17, 49) and
   246→  three `console.log` strings in `claude-handler.ts` (lines 100, 119, 179). Not runtime bugs.
   247→- Test describe blocks in `claude-handler.test.ts` still use old names: `handleClaudeQuery` (line 144),
   248→  `handleClaudeCancel` (line 768), `handleClaudeUpdatePermissionMode` (line 798).
   249→
   250→## HTTP vs WS Query Parity Gap (PR "Create Workspace from PR or Branch")
   251→
   252→- `GET /workspaces/by-repo` HTTP fallback and query-engine `runQuery("workspaces")` both build `RepoGroup[]` independently.
   253→  When adding a field to one, must add to both. Confirmed: `git_origin_url` added to query-engine but NOT to HTTP route group object.
   254→- Full details in `http-vs-ws-parity.md`
   255→
   256→## createWorkspace Command Return Value (Pre-Existing Bug, #confirmed)
   257→
   258→- `WorkspaceService.create()` returns `as unknown as Workspace` — but the WS ack only has `{ accepted, commandId }`.
   259→- `workspace.id` on the result is `undefined`. `selectWorkspace(undefined)` no-ops silently.
   260→- Pre-existing since bad96ad4 (protocol unification). Does not cause visible breakage because WS subscription pushes snapshot.
   261→- Full details in `create-workspace-command-return.md`
   262→
   263→## Connection State Machine (features/connection)
   264→
   265→- Zustand store in `connectionStore.ts`: CONNECTED → GRACE_PERIOD (2s) → RECONNECTING (30s total) → DISCONNECTED
   266→- `onDisconnected` guard checks `current !== "connected"` — must allow re-entry from `disconnected`
   267→  to handle Retry button flow (forceReconnect fires notifyConnectionChange(false) before new socket opens)
   268→- `forceReconnect()` in platform/ws immediately calls `notifyConnectionChange(false)` — if guard blocks,
   269→  the banner freezes at DISCONNECTED with no recovery until socket connects
   270→- `emitSendAttemptFailed` in `connectionEvents.ts` is a module-level Set-based event bus (no React deps)
   271→- WS error messages that trigger `emitSendAttemptFailed`: both `"not connected"` AND `"disconnected"`
   272→  must be matched — `"WebSocket disconnected"` (from onclose pending-command rejection) will be missed
   273→  if only checking `"not connected"`
   274→- `platform/ws → connectionStore` is safe (no circular import). `session.queries → connectionEvents` is safe.
   275→- `ConnectionBanner` animates `height` (violates project guidelines) — causes panel jitter on 32→44 change
   276→- `ConnectionOrb` color crossfade: CSS `transition-colors` on a class swap is better than Framer Motion
   277→  `backgroundColor` interpolation (avoids paint-per-frame)
   278→
   279→## Mobile Web Layout Patterns
   280→
   281→- Full details in `mobile-layout-patterns.md`
   282→- `MobileLayout` has `key={selectedWorkspace.id}` at call site in MainContent.tsx — remounts on workspace switch, resetting `activeTab` state
   283→- `MobileTab` type defined once in MobileTabBar.tsx, imported in MobileLayout.tsx
   284→- `AllFilesDiffViewer` in mobile code view uses `hideHeader` prop to suppress close button
   285→- `MobileTabBar` safe-area-inset: uses `pb-[env(...)]` which compresses content; needs `min-h` adjustment
   286→- `MobileTabBar` has ARIA semantics: `role="tablist"`, `role="tab"`, `aria-selected`, `aria-controls`, `aria-labelledby`
   287→
   288→## parseContent Type Contract (Confirmed)
   289→
   290→- `parseContent` from `useSessionWithMessages` has type `(content: string) => (ContentBlock | string)[] | string`
   291→  — NOT `ContentBlock[]`. Returns `string` for plain-text user messages and envelope-format cancelled messages.
   292→- Any utility that accepts `parseContent` as a parameter MUST declare the full return type and guard
   293→  with `Array.isArray` before iterating. Declaring a narrower type (`ContentBlock[]`) compiles but
   294→  causes silent data loss at runtime (string is iterated character-by-character, all `isToolUseBlock`
   295→  guards return false).
   296→- Standard guard pattern: `const result = parseContent(msg.content); if (!Array.isArray(result)) continue;`
   297→
   298→## Reverse-Traverse + Flip Pattern for Message Extraction
   299→
   300→- Walking messages in reverse (newest→oldest) and collecting chunks, then calling `.reverse()` to
   301→  restore chronological order is correct at the message level.
   302→- When also walking blocks within each message in reverse, intra-message block order is ALSO reversed
   303→  before the final flip — resulting in wrong per-message block order after `.reverse()`.
   304→- Correct pattern: walk messages in reverse, but walk BLOCKS within each message FORWARD. Either
   305→  `unshift` message-level results to the output array, or accumulate per-message and prepend.
   306→
   307→## TOOL_COLORS Map Completeness
   308→
   309→- Every tool renderer registered in `registerTools.ts` should have a corresponding entry in `TOOL_COLORS`
   310→  in `toolColors.ts`. Missing entries don't cause crashes but leave other components that derive color
   311→  by tool name with `undefined` (falls through to no class).
   312→- When adding a new renderer, add its color entry to `TOOL_COLORS` in the same commit.
   313→
   314→## Radix DropdownMenuTrigger + asChild + onClick Conflict Pattern
   315→
   316→- When `<DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>` wraps a child with its own `onClick`, Radix's Slot merges
   317→  BOTH handlers. If the child's `onClick` calls `setOpen(false)` (to suppress the dropdown for a
   318→  quick-open action), the dropdown STILL opens first (Radix toggle runs), then closes — visible flicker.
   319→- Fix: either don't use `DropdownMenuTrigger` on the quick-open button (use a sibling trigger for
   320→  the chevron only), or put the `DropdownMenuTrigger` on the chevron/separator button only.
   321→
   322→## Last-Used App Persistence Pattern
   323→
   324→- `useLastOpenInApp` / `getLastOpenInAppId` / `setLastOpenInAppId` live in
   325→  `apps/web/src/shared/hooks/useLastOpenInApp.ts` and are exported from `shared/hooks/index.ts`.
   326→- Pattern: `setLastAppId(appId)` must be called at EVERY `openIn(appId, path)` call site.
   327→  `WorkspaceHeader.tsx`'s `HeaderOpenButton` currently skips this call — Cmd+O will not reflect
   328→  choices made from that dropdown.
   329→
   330→## Spurious useEffect Deps Pattern
   331→
   332→- `useKeyboardShortcuts` includes `selectedWorkspace` and `modalStates` in its `useEffect` deps
   333→  array but neither is read inside the effect body. This causes the global keydown listener to
   334→  be torn down and re-registered on every workspace switch and modal state change.
   335→  Fix: remove those deps, or move logic that needs them into the callback params.
   336→
   337→## getInstalledAppsList() Race / Cache Pattern (native-handlers.ts)
   338→
   339→- Pre-fix risk: `cachedInstalledApps` alone allowed simultaneous first-load callers to duplicate the
   340→  full `mdfind` loop and race on shared temp icon paths.
   341→- Current pattern: pair `cachedInstalledApps` with `inflightPromise` so concurrent callers share the
   342→  same discovery pass until the cache is populated. `.finally()` clears `inflightPromise` on both
   343→  success and failure paths.
   344→
   345→## mdfind bundleId Query Safety
   346→
   347→- `mdfind` in `native-handlers.ts` interpolates `app.bundleId` directly into the query string:
   348→  `kMDItemCFBundleIdentifier == '${app.bundleId}'`. Values come from SUPPORTED_APPS which is
   349→  hardcoded, so this is safe. No user-controlled data reaches mdfind.
   350→
   351→## See Also
   352→
   353→- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, agent-server resume
   354→- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns
   355→

195→- The `useFileChanges` call inside `ContentView` (for the Files tab) is a leak of this pattern.
   196→  TanStack Query deduplicates the network call, so it is not a perf issue — but ownership is wrong.
   197→  Fix: move into the Files tab branch or into `FileBrowserPanel` itself.
   198→
   199→## Always-Mount CSS Hide Pattern (Confirmed)
   200→
   201→- Browser, Terminal, and Simulator panels use `pointer-events-none invisible absolute` (not `hidden`)
   202→  when inactive — keeps DOM alive so PTY/WebSocket sessions survive tab switches.
   203→- Pattern: `<div className={cn("h-full w-full", activeTab !== "terminal" && "pointer-events-none invisible absolute")}>`
   204→- `offsetParent === null` check in ResizeObserver guards against `fitAddon.fit()` being called when
   205→  terminal container is CSS-hidden (`invisible` sets `visibility: hidden`, which makes `offsetParent` null for
   206→  absolutely-positioned elements but NOT for `invisible` alone — `invisible absolute` together does make
   207→  `offsetParent` null in most cases, but it is fragile; the `offsetWidth/offsetHeight === 0` check is more reliable).
   208→- `partialize` in the layout store strips terminal tab state (tabs/activeId/nextNum) — PTY processes
   209→  don't survive app restarts; zombie tab metadata would reference dead processes.
   210→- The `updateTabs` helper in TerminalPanel captures `nextTerminalNum` from the _render_ snapshot —
   211→  stale-closure risk exists in the `pendingTask`/`pendingCommand` useEffect callbacks because
   212→  `tabs` and `nextTerminalNum` are excluded from their deps arrays (suppressed with eslint-disable).
   213→
   214→## Multi-Workspace Terminal Persistence Pattern (TerminalPanel.tsx)
   215→
   216→- `visitedWorkspaces: Map<workspaceId, workspacePath>` in TerminalPanel state keeps one
   217→  `WorkspaceTerminals` subtree mounted per visited workspace — PTYs survive workspace switches.
   218→- Non-current workspace terminals use same `pointer-events-none invisible absolute h-full w-full`
   219→  CSS-hide pattern inside a `relative` container.
   220→- `WorkspaceTerminals` sub-component uses per-workspace Zustand selectors for isolation.
   221→- Known leak: `visitedWorkspaces` has no eviction path when a workspace is deleted. PTYs for
   222→  deleted workspaces stay alive until TerminalPanel unmounts. Needs workspace deletion event hook.
   223→- `pendingTask` useEffect risk: if task queued just before workspace switch, the tab opens in the
   224→  NEW workspace because `workspaceId` in deps reflects the current workspace at effect fire time.

input.new_string:
## Hover-Intent Pattern (Confirmed Anti-Patterns)

- Shared timeout between strip and panel for hover-intent is valid, but `showPanel` MUST be read via
  a ref (not closure) inside `handleMouseEnter` to avoid stale-closure flicker when the panel is
  already open and the user re-enters the trigger.
- Pattern: `const showPanelRef = useRef(showPanel); useEffect(() => { showPanelRef.current = showPanel; }, [showPanel]);`
  Then `handleMouseEnter` has empty dep array and reads `showPanelRef.current`.
- Unmount cleanup is mandatory: both `enterTimeoutRef` and `leaveTimeoutRef` must be cleared in a
  `useEffect(() => () => { clearTimeout(...); clearTimeout(...); }, [])` — otherwise timeouts fire
  against stale state after workspace navigation.

## Absolute Panel Clipping Pattern (Recurring Risk)

- `absolute right-full` (or `left-full`) floating panels are clipped by ANY `overflow-hidden` ancestor,
  regardless of `z-index`. This is a common gotcha for popovers/flyouts rendered inside scroll containers.
- `ChangesView`'s root has `overflow-hidden`; minimap panel positioned with `absolute right-full` inside
  it will be invisible. Fix: React Portal (`createPortal`) positioned relative to viewport, OR add
  `overflow: visible` only to the flex wrapper that wraps both the diff viewer and the minimap strip.
- Always audit `overflow-hidden` ancestors when an `absolute`-positioned panel clips unexpectedly.

## FileChange Path Normalization (Confirmed Duplication)

- `fc.file || fc.file_path || ""` appears in AllFilesDiffViewer (scroll-spy loop), DiffFilesTree
  (groupByFolder), and FileChangeMinimap (filePath helper). Should be extracted to
  `features/workspace/lib/fileChange.ts` as `getFilePath(fc: FileChange): string`.
- `getChangeStatus(fc)` → "added"/"modified"/"deleted" is also duplicated between DiffFilesTree and
  FileChangeMinimap. Same extraction target.

## See Also

- `patterns-deep.md` — overflow notes: error classification, chat virtualization, PRStatus, border radius, agent-server resume
- `message-envelope-pattern.md` — session message envelope/flat array patterns

message.content: But we need also ability to expand and not have this minimap but like full thing, right? Now when we hover over the minimap we get full file tree of the changes but like we need the ability to have it there also to switch between this.

content.thinking:
The user wants the ability to toggle between two modes:
1. **Collapsed mode** (current minimap) — thin strip + hover popover
2. **Expanded mode** — the full file tree permanently visible (like the old ResizablePanelGroup layout)

So we need a toggle mechanism. Let me think about how to implement this cleanly.

The toggle could be:
- A small pin/expand button in the hover panel header (click to pin it open permanently)
- A button in the ChangesView header bar
- Both

I think the most intuitive UX is:


1. A pin icon in the hover panel header — click to "pin" the panel open (switch to expanded mode)
2. When expanded, a collapse icon to go back to minimap mode
3. Persist the state per workspace in the layout store

Now I'm weighing two implementation approaches: either keep both layouts in ChangesView and toggle between them (minimap mode with the current flex layout versus expanded mode with ResizablePanelGroup), or have FileChangeMinimap itself manage both states internally. The first approach seems cleaner for separation of concerns.

I'll add a `fileTreePinned` boolean to the workspace layout store, so when the user pins the panel, ChangesView switches from rendering the minimap to rendering the full ResizablePanelGroup layout. The toggle buttons go in the hover panel header for pinning and in the expanded tree header for collapsing back. button

For the ChangesView header, we could also add a toggle icon there.

Let me implement this:

1. Add `fileTreePinned: boolean` to the workspace layout store (defaults to `false`)
2. Add a `setFileTreePinned` action
3. In `ChangesView`, read this state and render either:
   - Pinned: ResizablePanelGroup layout (bring back the old layout)
   - Unpinned: Flex layout with FileChangeMinimap
4. In `FileChangeMinimap`, add a pin button in the hover panel header
5. In `DiffFilesTree` (or the panel wrapper in the pinned layout), add an unpin/collapse button

Actually, let me keep it simpler. Rather than adding to DiffFilesTree, I can create a wrapper in ChangesView for the pinned state that includes the header with the collapse button.

Or even simpler — I can add the toggle to the ChangesView header bar (next to the filter dropdown and review button). A small icon that toggles between minimap and expanded file tree. Plus, in the minimap hover panel, a pin button that switches to expanded mode.

Now I'm thinking through the store changes. I need to add a `fileTreePinned` boolean to the workspace layout state with a default of false, expose it through the hook, and then conditionally render either the minimap or the resizable panel layout in ChangesView based on that state.

content.text:
Right — you need a toggle between minimap mode and pinned expanded file tree. Here's the plan:

1. Add `fileTreePinned` state to the workspace layout store (persisted per workspace)
2. `ChangesView` conditionally renders minimap or resizable panel layout
3. Hover panel gets a pin button → switches to expanded
4. Expanded panel gets a collapse button → switches back to minimap

Let me implement.

content.text:
All clean. Here's the summary of what's done:

## Changes

### New: `FileChangeMinimap.tsx`
- Thin strip (24px) of colored lines — green/yellow/red per file change status
- Hover-intent panel (150ms enter delay, 200ms leave delay) with `DiffFilesTree`
- Portal-rendered to escape `overflow-hidden` ancestors
- Pin button in hover panel header → switches to expanded mode

### Modified: `ChangesView.tsx`
- Now toggles between two layouts based on `fileTreePinned` state:
  - **Minimap mode** (default): flex layout with `AllFilesDiffViewer` (full width) + `FileChangeMinimap`
  - **Pinned mode**: `ResizablePanelGroup` (75/25) with permanent `DiffFilesTree` sidebar
- Pinned panel has a header with file count + collapse button (`PanelRightClose` icon) to switch back to minimap

### Modified: `workspaceLayoutStore.ts` + `useWorkspaceLayout.ts`
- Added `fileTreePinned: boolean` (default `false`) to per-workspace layout state
- Exposed `setFileTreePinned` callback in the hook
- Persisted to localStorage — remembers each workspace's preference

### Modified: `AllFilesDiffViewer.tsx`
- Added `className` prop for flex sizing when in minimap mode

message.content: Also, when I'm with like a drag when I have a run, I'm trying to resize the file changes file tree. I should be able to drag it into the minimap collapse state, you know.

content.thinking:
The user wants to be able to drag the resize handle to collapse the file tree panel all the way down to the minimap state. So when in pinned mode, if you drag the resize handle far enough to the right, it should transition from the pinned resizable layout into the minimap mode.

The `react-resizable-panels` library supports `collapsible` prop on panels. When a panel is collapsed via drag, it fires `onCollapse` callback. I can use this to switch from pinned to minimap mode.

Let me look at how `react-resizable-panels` handles collapsible panels:
- `collapsible` prop on `ResizablePanel`


- `collapsedSize` sets the size when collapsed (typically 0)
- `onCollapse` and `onExpand` callbacks fire when the panel state changes

So I'll add the `collapsible` prop to the file tree panel with `collapsedSize={0}`, and when `onCollapse` fires, I'll switch to minimap mode by setting `setFileTreePinned(false)`. This way, dragging the resize handle far enough to the right will automatically collapse the panel and transition into minimap mode.

Now I'm implementing this.

content.text: Great idea — drag the resize handle far enough right and it snaps to minimap mode. `react-resizable-panels` has built-in `collapsible` support for exactly this.

content.text:
Yeah that makes sense — clicking a line in the minimap strip currently scrolls to that file, but it could also pin the panel open at the same time. And clicking a file in the hover panel could do the same: navigate to the file AND pin the tree open. That way any deliberate click interaction = "I want to work with this file tree."

Let me wire that up.

The `handleFileClick` already scrolls to the file. I just need to also pin the tree when a file is clicked from the minimap. The cleanest way: add an `onFileClick` wrapper in `FileChangeMinimap` that calls both `onFileClick` and `onPin`.
I'll make clicks on both the strip lines and the hover panel file tree pin the panel open:

content.text:
Yes — it's fully wired:

1. **Scroll-spy** in `AllFilesDiffViewer` detects which file is at the top → calls `workspaceLayoutActions.setSelectedFilePath()`
2. **ChangesView** reads `selectedFilePath` from `useWorkspaceLayout`
3. **FileChangeMinimap** receives it as `selectedFile` prop
4. The minimap line for the active file renders wider (`w-3.5`) and full opacity vs the others (`w-2.5 opacity-60`)

So as you scroll through diffs, the highlighted minimap line already follows. This should be working out of the box — you'll see it when you test visually.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive exploration report.

## Workspace UI Files: Complete Exploration

### Overview of All Files

The workspace UI directory contains **15 files** focused on displaying and managing file changes, diffs, and workspace content. Here's a complete breakdown:

| File | Purpose | Exports |
|------|---------|---------|
| `ChangesView.tsx` | Main changes/diffs tab — orchestrates all diff viewing components | `ChangesView` (component) |
| `AllFilesDiffViewer.tsx` | Scrollable container for all changed files' diffs with lazy loading | `AllFilesDiffViewer` (forwardRef), `AllFilesDiffViewerRef` (type) |
| `AllDiffFileSection.tsx` | Per-file section component — renders individual diff + header | `AllDiffFileSection` (memoized component) |
| `DiffViewer.tsx` | Core diff renderer powered by @pierre/diffs library | `DiffViewer` (component) |
| `DiffFilesTree.tsx` | Compact file tree showing only changed files | `DiffFilesTree` (component) |
| `FileChangeMinimap.tsx` | Thin colored strip showing files, expands to panel on hover | `FileChangeMinimap` (component) |
| `FilesView.tsx` | File browser tab — two-panel file viewer + tree | `FilesView` (component) |
| `MainContentTabs.tsx` | Chat session tab bar with drag-to-reorder and restore history | `MainContentTabBar` (component), `Tab` (type), `ClosedTab` (type) |
| `WorkspaceHeader.tsx` | Top bar — branch selector, PR actions, run button | `WorkspaceHeader` (component) |
| `BranchSelector.tsx` | Dropdown to select/create branches | `BranchSelector` (component) |
| `PRActions.tsx` | Pull request UI (create, open, status, etc.) | `PRActions` (component) |
| `HeaderRunButton.tsx` | "Run" button for workspace — spawns new session | `HeaderRunButton` (component) |
| `DesignPanel.tsx` | Design file preview (Penpot/Figma integration) | `DesignPanel` (component) |
| `SortableTab.tsx` | Draggable tab wrapper using dnd-kit | `SortableTab` (component) |
| `WorkspaceHeader.stories.tsx` | Storybook stories for WorkspaceHeader | Story exports (Default, WithTitle, SetupRunning, etc.) |

---

### Component Hierarchy & Relationships

```
ChangesView (Main orchestrator)
├── AllFilesDiffViewer (Large scrollable list)
│   └── AllDiffFileSection (Per-file, memoized)
│       └── DiffViewer (Low-level @pierre/diffs renderer)
├── DiffFilesTree (Sidebar file list — two modes)
│   └── FolderGroupView (Subfolder grouping)
│        └── [File buttons]
└── FileChangeMinimap (Thin strip + floating panel)
    ├── [Colored lines strip]
    └── [Portal-rendered panel]
        └── DiffFilesTree (Reused via hover)

FilesView (Files tab)
├── FileViewer (left panel — file preview)
└── FileBrowserPanel (right panel — file tree)

MainContentTabs (Chat area)
├── SortableTab (Draggable wrapper)
└── [Tab items with icons, close buttons]

WorkspaceHeader (Top bar)
├── BranchSelector (Branch dropdown)
├── PRActions (PR UI)
└── HeaderRunButton (Run session)
```

---

### Naming Conventions Analysis

The naming is **mostly consistent** but shows some patterns worth noting:

#### Naming Patterns

**1. Component Prefixes (Mostly Consistent)**
- `DiffFilesTree` — diff-specific
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` — diff-specific, "All" prefix means all files
- `AllDiffFileSection` — diff-specific, per-section
- `DiffViewer` — diff-specific, low-level renderer
- `FileChangeMinimap` — file change specific
- `ChangesView` — high-level view container

**Naming Issues:**
- **Prefix Inconsistency**: `DiffFilesTree` vs `FileChangeMinimap` vs `ChangesView`
  - "Diff" vs "FileChange" vs "Changes" — three different prefixes for the same feature domain
  - `DiffFilesTree` should arguably be `DiffFilesPanel` (it's a tree in a panel, not a tree component)
  - Could be standardized to: `ChangesDiffFilesTree`, `ChangesDiffViewer`, `ChangesFilesPanel`

**2. Names Accurately Describe Function**
- ✅ `AllFilesDiffViewer` — accurately describes: shows all files' diffs
- ✅ `FileChangeMinimap` — accurately describes: minimap for file changes (thin strip)
- ✅ `AllDiffFileSection` — accurately describes: one section per file in all-diffs view
- ✅ `DiffViewer` — accurately describes: renders diffs (generic, reusable)
- ✅ `DiffFilesTree` — accurately describes: tree of changed files (mostly; see above)

**3. Ref Types**
- `AllFilesDiffViewerRef` — exported separately, good naming for imperative methods (`scrollToFile`)

---

### Key Features of Each Component

#### **1. ChangesView.tsx** (8.5 KB, Modified Apr 2 15:08)

**Main Export:** `ChangesView` component

**Purpose:** Container component for the "Changes" tab. Manages layout modes and data flow.

**Props:**
- `workspace: Workspace`
- `isWatched?: boolean` — disables polling when file watcher is active
- `onReview?: () => void` — callback to insert code review prompt
- `compact?: boolean` — mobile mode (single column)

**Three Layout Modes (Persisted per workspace):**
1. **Minimap (default)** — Full-width diff viewer + thin strip on right, expands to panel on hover
2. **Pinned** — Resizable two-panel: diff viewer + permanent file tree sidebar
3. **Compact (mobile)** — Single column, diff viewer only

**Child Components:**
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` — core diff viewer (ref-based for scroll control)
- `DiffFilesTree` — file tree when pinned
- `FileChangeMinimap` — colored strip + hover panel

**State Management:**
- `useWorkspaceLayout(workspace.id)` — per-workspace UI state (selected file, pinned status)
- `useFileChanges()` — fetches file change data
- Local state: `changesFilter` (currently unused, "all-changes" hardcoded)

**Key Patterns:**
- Filter dropdown (with TODO for uncommitted/last-turn filters)
- "Review Changes" button (contextual, only when files exist)
- Scroll-to-file coordination between diff viewer and file tree via ref

---

#### **2. AllFilesDiffViewer.tsx** (9.4 KB, Modified Apr 2 14:56)

**Main Exports:**
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` (forwardRef)
- `AllFilesDiffViewerRef` (type with `scrollToFile` method)

**Purpose:** Scrollable container for all changed files' diffs. Implements scroll-spy to track active file.

**Props:**
- `workspaceId: string`
- `fileChanges: FileChange[]`
- `hideHeader?: boolean` — used when embedded in ChangesView
- `onActiveFileChange?: (filePath: string | null) => void` — callback when active file changes
- `initialScrollTarget?: string` — one-shot scroll on mount
- `className?: string`

**Key Implementation Details:**
- **Lazy Loading via IntersectionObserver** — 600px rootMargin (load sections before they appear)
- **Scroll-Spy:** rAF-throttled scroll listener finds topmost visible section using 1/3 threshold
- **Ref Map:** Maintains map of file paths → DOM elements for O(1) scroll-to-file
- **Expand/Collapse Global:** Counter-based key forces all sections to reset to expanded state

**Performance Optimizations:**
- Uses refs to avoid re-creating callbacks on prop change
- Memoized `AllDiffFileSection` children
- `content-visibility: auto` friendly via centralized scroll listener (not per-section IntersectionObserver)

**Imperative Ref Interface:**
```typescript
{
  scrollToFile(path: string): void  // Immediate active file update + smooth scroll
}
```

---

#### **3. AllDiffFileSection.tsx** (5.3 KB)

**Main Export:** `AllDiffFileSection` (memoized)

**Purpose:** Per-file section component. Sticky file header with collapse toggle + lazy-loaded diff content.

**Props:**
- `workspaceId: string`
- `fileChange: FileChange` — one file's change metadata
- `isActive: boolean` — whether this section is currently being viewed
- `sectionRef: (filePath, HTMLElement | null) => void` — callback to register DOM element
- `expandStateKey: number` — counter to reset expand state globally

**Key Implementation:**
- **Sticky Header** — shows file path, +N/-N stats, collapse toggle
- **Lazy Diff Loading** — `useFileDiff()` only fetches when `isNearVisible` (IntersectionObserver 600px margin)
- **Collapse State Logic:** Tracked locally with key counter; resets to expanded when parent's `expandStateKey` increments
- **Memo Comparison:** Custom equality check (path, additions, deletions, isActive, workspaceId, expandStateKey)

---

#### **4. DiffViewer.tsx** (21 KB)

**Main Export:** `DiffViewer` component

**Purpose:** Low-level unified diff renderer powered by `@pierre/diffs` library.

**Props:**
- `filePath?: string`
- `diff?: string` — raw unified diff text
- `oldContent?: string | null` — full old file (enables expand-all)
- `newContent?: string | null` — full new file (enables expand-all)
- `isLoading?: boolean`
- `error?: string | null`
- `onClose?: () => void`
- `embedded?: boolean` — when true, natural height + no internal scroll (for stacking in parent)
- `workspaceId?: string` — for sending diff comments to chat

**Advanced Features:**
- **Two Rendering Paths:**
  1. **Full File** (oldContent + newContent available) → `parseDiffFromFile()` for expandable hunks
  2. **Patch Only** (diff text only) → `getSingularPatch()` fallback
- **Diff Comments:** Draft/saved comments on lines with send-to-chat action
- **Mobile Interactions:** Long-press (500ms) to open comment, tap to select line
- **Large Diff Handling:** Disables word-level diffs above 2000 lines
- **Show All:** Toggle to expand collapsed unchanged lines (when full file context available)
- **Copy Diff:** Clipboard action in header

**Diff Parsing Helpers:**
- `extractDiffPaths()` — extracts old/new file paths from diff headers
- `applyDisplayNames()` — shows only basename for display
- `normalizeGitPath()` — strips `a/` and `b/` prefixes from git paths

---

#### **5. DiffFilesTree.tsx** (5.6 KB, Modified Apr 2 15:45)

**Main Export:** `DiffFilesTree` component

**Purpose:** Compact file tree showing only changed files. Groups by folder, shows status dots and stats.

**Props:**
- `fileChanges: FileChange[]`
- `selectedFile: string | null` — which file is currently active
- `onFileClick: (path: string) => void` — callback to scroll diff viewer to file

**Implementation:**
- **Folder Grouping:** Groups files by directory using `groupByFolder()` helper
- **Folder Collapse:** Stateful collapsing per folder
- **Status Indicator:** Color dots (green=added, yellow=modified, red=deleted) via `STATUS_BG` from utils
- **Stats Display:** Shows +N/-N additions/deletions per file (monospace, right-aligned)
- **Root Files:** Files in project root rendered without indentation

**Helper Components:**
- `FolderGroupView()` — renders one folder group with collapsible header + file items

---

#### **6. FileChangeMinimap.tsx** (6.7 KB, Modified Apr 2 15:46, NEW FILE)

**Main Export:** `FileChangeMinimap` component

**Purpose:** Thin vertical strip (~24px) showing one colored line per changed file. Expands to floating panel on hover.

**Props:**
- `fileChanges: FileChange[]`
- `selectedFile: string | null`
- `onFileClick: (path: string) => void`
- `onPin: () => void` — callback to switch to pinned layout

**Implementation Details:**
- **Strip Rendering:** Vertical stack of thin colored lines (2-3px), gaps between
- **Hover Intent Pattern:** 
  - Enter delay: 150ms before panel appears
  - Leave delay: 200ms before panel disappears
  - Prevents flicker when moving between strip and panel
- **Portal-Rendered Panel:** Fixed position via `createPortal()` to escape ancestor `overflow-hidden`
- **Framer Motion Animation:** Fade + slide-right on enter/exit
- **Line Selection State:** Wider/more opaque when selected
- **Pin Button:** In panel header to switch to pinned mode

**Animation Config:**
```typescript
ease-out-quart: cubic-bezier(.165, .84, .44, 1)
duration: 150ms
```

---

#### **7. FilesView.tsx** (2.4 KB)

**Main Export:** `FilesView` component

**Purpose:** Alternative view for the "Files" tab — full workspace file browser with preview.

**Props:**
- `workspace: Workspace`
- `isWatched?: boolean`

**Two-Panel Layout:**
- **Left (75%):** `FileViewer` — preview of selected file
- **Right (25%):** `FileBrowserPanel` — full workspace file tree

**Key Difference from ChangesView:**
- Shows ALL files, not just changed files
- Uses `FileBrowserPanel` (full tree) instead of `DiffFilesTree` (filtered tree)
- Passes `fileChanges` to highlight modified files in the tree

---

#### **8. MainContentTabs.tsx** (12 KB)

**Main Exports:**
- `MainContentTabBar` (component)
- `Tab` (type)
- `ClosedTab` (type)

**Purpose:** Tab bar for chat sessions with drag-to-reorder, session status indicators, and restore history.

**Tabs Include:**
- Per-tab icon: spinner (working), gold dot (unread), or agent logo
- Per-tab close button (only when 2+ tabs)
- New tab button (+ icon)
- Restore history button (history icon, only when closed tabs exist)
- Collapse chat panel button (far right)

**Key Features:**
- **Drag-to-Reorder:** Uses dnd-kit with mouse + touch support
- **Working/Unread Status:** Per-tab spinners and unread dots
- **Close Rules:** Any tab can be closed as long as ≥1 remains
- **Restore Popover:** "Recently Closed" list with timestamp info

---

### Naming Inconsistencies & Recommendations

#### Problem 1: Prefix Mismatch
Current: `Diff*`, `FileChange*`, `Changes*`
- `DiffFilesTree` — uses "Diff" prefix
- `FileChangeMinimap` — uses "FileChange" prefix
- `ChangesView` — uses "Changes" prefix (matches the tab name)
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` — uses "AllFiles" + "Diff" prefix

**Recommendation:** Standardize to one of:
- Option A (Changes): `ChangesFilesPanel` (instead of `DiffFilesTree`), `ChangesMinimap` (instead of `FileChangeMinimap`)
- Option B (Diff): `DiffFilesPanel`, `DiffMinimap`
- Option C (Combined): `ChangesView` → `DiffChangesView` for clarity

#### Problem 2: Tree vs Panel Naming
`DiffFilesTree` — is actually a scrollable panel component, not a tree component
- Could be `DiffFilesPanel` (more accurate)
- Or `DiffFilesList` (emphasizes vertical layout)

#### Problem 3: Unclear "All" Semantics
`AllFilesDiffViewer` — the "All" prefix means "all changed files in this workspace"
- This is clear in context but could be `WorkspaceDiffViewer` for explicitness

---

### Props & Data Flow Patterns

**File Change Data:**
All components derive from the `FileChange` type (shared):
```typescript
FileChange {
  file?: string          // file path (inconsistently named)
  file_path?: string     // backup field for file path
  additions: number
  deletions: number
  status?: string        // "added" | "modified" | "deleted"
}
```

**Shared Utilities** (workspace.utils.ts):
- `getChangeStatus(change)` — derives status from additions/deletions
- `STATUS_BG` — maps status → Tailwind color class
- `fileChangePath(fc)` — safely extracts file path (handles both `file` and `file_path`)

**Layout State** (useWorkspaceLayout hook):
- `selectedFilePath` — which file is currently being viewed
- `fileTreePinned` — whether pinned mode is active
- Per-workspace persistence

---

### Performance Characteristics

| Component | Rendering | Data Fetching |
|-----------|-----------|---------------|
| `ChangesView` | Fast (orchestrator) | Bulk fetch via `useFileChanges()` |
| `AllFilesDiffViewer` | O(n) sections, each memoized | Lazy via IntersectionObserver (600px margin) |
| `AllDiffFileSection` | Memoized, re-renders only on change | Conditional `useFileDiff()` when near-visible |
| `DiffViewer` | Fast (library handles rendering) | Direct (passed in props) |
| `DiffFilesTree` | O(n) files in tree (scrollable) | Memoized grouping via `groupByFolder()` |
| `FileChangeMinimap` | O(n) lines + portal panel | Memoized via `useMemo()` |
| `FilesView` | Two panels, resizable | Same as ChangesView + full file tree |

**Optimization Patterns:**
- IntersectionObserver for lazy loading diffs
- rAF-throttled scroll-spy (centralized, not per-section)
- Memoized components with custom equality checks
- Ref-based forwarding to avoid re-renders
- Portal rendering for hover panel (escapes overflow ancestors)

---

### Summary of Key Insights

1. **Clear Separation of Concerns:**
   - `ChangesView` = orchestrator
   - `AllFilesDiffViewer` = list container
   - `AllDiffFileSection` = per-item section
   - `DiffViewer` = reusable diff renderer

2. **Layout Modes:**
   - Three persisted modes: Minimap (default), Pinned, Compact
   - User can toggle between them without losing state

3. **Naming Issues:**
   - Mix of "Diff", "FileChange", "Changes" prefixes (should standardize)
   - "Tree" name for `DiffFilesTree` is slightly misleading (it's a panel component)

4. **Performance Optimizations:**
   - Lazy loading with 600px margin (great UX)
   - Centralized scroll-spy (avoids per-section IntersectionObserver)
   - Memoization with precise equality checks
   - Portal-based hover panel (doesn't trigger scroll ancestor reflow)

5. **Component Reusability:**
   - `DiffFilesTree` used in both Pinned mode and Minimap hover panel (good DRY)
   - `DiffViewer` standalone and embedded (flexible `embedded` prop)

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive exploration report.

## Workspace UI Files: Complete Exploration

### Overview of All Files

The workspace UI directory contains **15 files** focused on displaying and managing file changes, diffs, and workspace content. Here's a complete breakdown:

| File | Purpose | Exports |
|------|---------|---------|
| `ChangesView.tsx` | Main changes/diffs tab — orchestrates all diff viewing components | `ChangesView` (component) |
| `AllFilesDiffViewer.tsx` | Scrollable container for all changed files' diffs with lazy loading | `AllFilesDiffViewer` (forwardRef), `AllFilesDiffViewerRef` (type) |
| `AllDiffFileSection.tsx` | Per-file section component — renders individual diff + header | `AllDiffFileSection` (memoized component) |
| `DiffViewer.tsx` | Core diff renderer powered by @pierre/diffs library | `DiffViewer` (component) |
| `DiffFilesTree.tsx` | Compact file tree showing only changed files | `DiffFilesTree` (component) |
| `FileChangeMinimap.tsx` | Thin colored strip showing files, expands to panel on hover | `FileChangeMinimap` (component) |
| `FilesView.tsx` | File browser tab — two-panel file viewer + tree | `FilesView` (component) |
| `MainContentTabs.tsx` | Chat session tab bar with drag-to-reorder and restore history | `MainContentTabBar` (component), `Tab` (type), `ClosedTab` (type) |
| `WorkspaceHeader.tsx` | Top bar — branch selector, PR actions, run button | `WorkspaceHeader` (component) |
| `BranchSelector.tsx` | Dropdown to select/create branches | `BranchSelector` (component) |
| `PRActions.tsx` | Pull request UI (create, open, status, etc.) | `PRActions` (component) |
| `HeaderRunButton.tsx` | "Run" button for workspace — spawns new session | `HeaderRunButton` (component) |
| `DesignPanel.tsx` | Design file preview (Penpot/Figma integration) | `DesignPanel` (component) |
| `SortableTab.tsx` | Draggable tab wrapper using dnd-kit | `SortableTab` (component) |
| `WorkspaceHeader.stories.tsx` | Storybook stories for WorkspaceHeader | Story exports (Default, WithTitle, SetupRunning, etc.) |

---

### Component Hierarchy & Relationships

```
ChangesView (Main orchestrator)
├── AllFilesDiffViewer (Large scrollable list)
│   └── AllDiffFileSection (Per-file, memoized)
│       └── DiffViewer (Low-level @pierre/diffs renderer)
├── DiffFilesTree (Sidebar file list — two modes)
│   └── FolderGroupView (Subfolder grouping)
│        └── [File buttons]
└── FileChangeMinimap (Thin strip + floating panel)
    ├── [Colored lines strip]
    └── [Portal-rendered panel]
        └── DiffFilesTree (Reused via hover)

FilesView (Files tab)
├── FileViewer (left panel — file preview)
└── FileBrowserPanel (right panel — file tree)

MainContentTabs (Chat area)
├── SortableTab (Draggable wrapper)
└── [Tab items with icons, close buttons]

WorkspaceHeader (Top bar)
├── BranchSelector (Branch dropdown)
├── PRActions (PR UI)
└── HeaderRunButton (Run session)
```

---

### Naming Conventions Analysis

The naming is **mostly consistent** but shows some patterns worth noting:

#### Naming Patterns

**1. Component Prefixes (Mostly Consistent)**
- `DiffFilesTree` — diff-specific
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` — diff-specific, "All" prefix means all files
- `AllDiffFileSection` — diff-specific, per-section
- `DiffViewer` — diff-specific, low-level renderer
- `FileChangeMinimap` — file change specific
- `ChangesView` — high-level view container

**Naming Issues:**
- **Prefix Inconsistency**: `DiffFilesTree` vs `FileChangeMinimap` vs `ChangesView`
  - "Diff" vs "FileChange" vs "Changes" — three different prefixes for the same feature domain
  - `DiffFilesTree` should arguably be `DiffFilesPanel` (it's a tree in a panel, not a tree component)
  - Could be standardized to: `ChangesDiffFilesTree`, `ChangesDiffViewer`, `ChangesFilesPanel`

**2. Names Accurately Describe Function**
- ✅ `AllFilesDiffViewer` — accurately describes: shows all files' diffs
- ✅ `FileChangeMinimap` — accurately describes: minimap for file changes (thin strip)
- ✅ `AllDiffFileSection` — accurately describes: one section per file in all-diffs view
- ✅ `DiffViewer` — accurately describes: renders diffs (generic, reusable)
- ✅ `DiffFilesTree` — accurately describes: tree of changed files (mostly; see above)

**3. Ref Types**
- `AllFilesDiffViewerRef` — exported separately, good naming for imperative methods (`scrollToFile`)

---

### Key Features of Each Component

#### **1. ChangesView.tsx** (8.5 KB, Modified Apr 2 15:08)

**Main Export:** `ChangesView` component

**Purpose:** Container component for the "Changes" tab. Manages layout modes and data flow.

**Props:**
- `workspace: Workspace`
- `isWatched?: boolean` — disables polling when file watcher is active
- `onReview?: () => void` — callback to insert code review prompt
- `compact?: boolean` — mobile mode (single column)

**Three Layout Modes (Persisted per workspace):**
1. **Minimap (default)** — Full-width diff viewer + thin strip on right, expands to panel on hover
2. **Pinned** — Resizable two-panel: diff viewer + permanent file tree sidebar
3. **Compact (mobile)** — Single column, diff viewer only

**Child Components:**
- `AllFilesDiffViewer` — core diff viewer (ref-based for scroll control)
- `DiffFilesTree` — file tree when pinned
- `FileChangeMinimap` — colored strip + hover panel

**State Management:**
- `useWorkspaceLayout(workspace.id)` — per-workspace UI state (selected file, pinned status)
- `useFileChanges()` — fetches file change data
- Local state: `changesFilter` (currently unused, "all-changes" hardcoded)

**Key Patterns:**
- Filter dropdown (with TODO for uncommitted/last-turn filters)
- "Review Changes" button (contextual, only when files exist)
- Scroll-to-file coordination between diff viewer and file tree via ref

---

#### **2. AllFilesDiffViewer.tsx** (9.4 KB, Modified Apr 2 14:56)

**Purpose:** Scrollable container for all changed files' diffs. Implements scroll-spy to track active file.

- `workspaceId: string`

**Key Implementation Details:**
- **Lazy Loading via IntersectionObserver** — 600px rootMargin (load sections before they appear)
- **Scroll-Spy:** rAF-throttled scroll listener finds topmost visible section using 1/3 threshold
- **Ref Map:** Maintains map of file paths → DOM elements for O(1) scroll-to-file
- **Expand/Collapse Global:** Counter-based key forces all sections to reset to expanded state

**Performance Optimizations:**
- Uses refs to avoid re-creating callbacks on prop change
- Memoized `AllDiffFileSection` children
- `content-visibility: auto` friendly via centralized scroll listener (not per-section IntersectionObserver)

**Imperative Ref Interface:**
```typescript
{
  scrollToFile(path: string): void  // Immediate active file update + smooth scroll
}
```

#### **3. AllDiffFileSection.tsx** (5.3 KB)

**Key Implementation:**

- **Collapse State Logic:** Tracked locally with key counter; resets to expanded when parent's `expandStateKey` increments
- **Memo Comparison:** Custom equality check (path, additions, deletions, isActive, workspaceId, expandStateKey)

#### **4. DiffViewer.tsx** (21 KB)

**Advanced Features:**

Here's what I found — the naming is inconsistent across three different prefixes for the same domain:

| Current Name | Prefix Used | What It Actually Is |
|---|---|---|
| `ChangesView` | "Changes" | Top-level orchestrator (matches tab name) |
| `AllFilesDiffViewer` | "AllFiles" + "Diff" | Scrollable list of all file diffs |
| `AllDiffFileSection` | "AllDiff" | Single file's diff section |
| `DiffFilesTree` | "Diff" | File tree sidebar panel |
| `DiffViewer` | "Diff" | Low-level diff renderer (generic, reusable) |
| `FileChangeMinimap` | "FileChange" | Minimap strip + hover panel |

Three different prefixes — "Changes", "Diff", "FileChange" — for the same feature area.

**My recommendation:** Standardize on **"Changes"** prefix (matches the tab name "Changes") for feature-level components, keep **"Diff"** only for the generic reusable renderer:

| Current | Proposed | Why |

content.text:
--- ## Review Report ### Critical Issues **[`useWorkspaceLayout.ts:154`] `fileTreePinned` fallback is `?? false` but `defaultLayout.fileTreePinned` is `true`** ```ts // Hook line 154: fileTreePinned: layout.fileTreePinned ?? false, // Store defaultLayout line 93: fileTreePinned: true, ``` After the store migrates (`migrate` wipes all layouts), every workspace record starts empty. `layout.fileTreePinned` is `undefined`, so `?? false` coerces it to `false` — landing users in minimap mode. The intent in `defaultLayout` is clearly to start pinned. All other fallbacks in this hook that differ from the raw field (e.g. `chatPanelCollapsed ?? false`) happen to agree with `defaultLayout` by coincidence. This one does not. Fix: ```ts fileTreePinned: layout.fileTreePinned ?? defaultLayout.fileTreePinned, ``` --- ### Warnings **[`ChangesMinimap.tsx:16`] Missing `useReducedMotion()` on the panel enter/exit animation** `ChangesMinimap` animates a panel with `panelTransition = { duration: 0.15 }` and `panelVariants` but does not call `useReducedMotion()`. The established pattern for every new animated component in this codebase (confirmed across `RepositoryItem`, `PastedImageCard`, `PastedTextCard`, `ServerOfflinePage`, `ConnectionBanner`, etc.) is to respect this preference: ```ts const reduceMotion = useReducedMotion(); const panelTransition = { duration: reduceMotion ? 0 : 0.15, ease: [0.165, 0.84, 0.44, 1] }; ``` **[`ChangesView.tsx:165`] Pinned panel header is inlined in `ChangesView` and duplicated in `ChangesMinimap`** The "Files" panel header (label + count badge + close/pin button) appears twice: inline at `ChangesView.tsx:170–187` and again inside `ChangesMinimap.tsx:155–171`. They are structurally identical. If the height (`h-8`), padding, or button style ever changes, both must be updated. A shared sub-component or a prop on `ChangesFilesPanel` (e.g. `header?: ReactNode`) would close this. **[`ChangesMinimap.tsx:174`] `h-[calc(100%-2rem)]` is fragile — should be a flex child** The floating panel's content div uses `h-[calc(100%-2rem)]` to fill the space below the `h-8` header. The outer `` already has `height` set to `panelRect.height` via inline style, so `flex flex-col` + `min-h-0 flex-1` would be self-healing and consistent with every other panel layout in the codebase: ```tsx
…
 ``` --- ### Suggestions **[`ChangesMinimap.tsx:56`] Panel position flashes at `top:0, right:0` on first open** `panelRect` initializes to all zeros. On the first `showPanel = true` render, the panel appears at the wrong position for one frame before the `useEffect` fires and corrects it. Computing the rect inside the `setTimeout` in `handleMouseEnter` (before calling `setShowPanel(true)`) avoids the two-render sequence and eliminates the flash entirely. **[`workspace.utils.ts:8–12`] `getChangeStatus` heuristic covers edge cases silently** A file with 0 additions AND 0 deletions (rename-only or chmod) resolves to `"modified"`. Worth a one-line comment noting the heuristic is intentional and that rename-only changes are treated as modified. --- ### What's Clean - All stale references to `AllFilesDiffViewer`, `AllDiffFileSection`, `DiffFilesTree`, `FileChangeMinimap` are gone. No dead imports. - The `ChangesDiffViewerRef` type is correctly threaded: defined in `ChangesDiffViewer.tsx`, exported via `index.ts`, imported in `ChangesView.tsx`. - `ChangesMinimap` correctly uses `m` (not `motion`) — the LazyMotion-compatible alias. - The `AnimatePresence` wrapping in `ChangesMinimap` follows the correct pattern: `{condition && }` — no early-return-before-AnimatePresence bug. - `createPortal(…, document.body)` correctly escapes the `overflow-hidden` ancestor chain — the portal risk flagged in reviewer memory is handled. - Hover-intent implementation in `ChangesMinimap` matches the confirmed pattern: shared timeouts, `showPanelRef` to avoid stale closures, both timeouts cleared on unmount. - `setFileTreePinned` in the hook goes through `setLayout` (the three-way merge path), not through an individual setter — avoids the two-way merge bug. - `fileChangePath`, `getChangeStatus`, `STATUS_BG` extraction to `workspace.utils.ts` resolves the duplication that was flagged in reviewer memory. **Summary: 1 critical, 3 warnings, 2 suggestions across 6 files.**

1→/**
     2→ * Workspace Layout Store
     3→ *
     4→ * Per-workspace layout state persistence for content panel, files, and browser.
     5→ * Each workspace remembers its own layout configuration independently.
     6→ *
     7→ * State Structure:
     8→ * {
     9→ *   [workspaceId]: {
    10→ *     activeContentTab: 'changes' | 'files' | 'config' | 'terminal' | ...
    11→ *     selectedFilePath: string | null
    12→ *     sidebarCollapsed: boolean
    13→ *     chatPanelCollapsed: boolean
    14→ *     contentPanelCollapsed: boolean
    15→ *   }
    16→ * }
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import { create } from "zustand";
    20→import { devtools, persist } from "zustand/middleware";
    21→import type { PersistedBrowserTab } from "@/features/browser/types";
    22→
    23→export type ContentTab =
    24→  | "changes"
    25→  | "files"
    26→  | "config"
    27→  | "terminal"
    28→  | "notebook"
    29→  | "design"
    30→  | "browser"
    31→  | "simulator";
    32→
    33→export interface PersistedTerminalTab {
    34→  id: string;
    35→  title: string;
    36→}
    37→
    38→interface WorkspaceLayoutState {
    39→  activeContentTab: ContentTab;
    40→  selectedFilePath: string | null;
    41→  sidebarCollapsed: boolean;
    42→  browserTabs: PersistedBrowserTab[]; // Persisted browser tab URLs/titles
    43→  activeBrowserTabId: string | null; // Which browser tab was last active
    44→  chatPanelCollapsed: boolean;
    45→  contentPanelCollapsed: boolean;
    46→  fileTreePinned: boolean; // true = expanded file tree panel, false = minimap strip + hover
    47→  chatTabSessionIds: string[]; // Ordered session IDs for open chat tabs
    48→  activeChatTabSessionId: string | null; // Which chat tab is active
    49→  pendingTerminalCommand: string | null; // Command to auto-run in a new terminal tab (e.g. "claude login")
    50→  simulatorUdid: string | null; // Last-used simulator UDID for this workspace
    51→  terminalTabs: PersistedTerminalTab[]; // Per-workspace terminal tab metadata
    52→  activeTerminalTabId: string | null; // Which terminal tab is active
    53→  nextTerminalNum: number; // Counter for "Terminal N" naming
    54→}
    55→
    56→interface WorkspaceLayoutStore {
    57→  layouts: Record<string, WorkspaceLayoutState>;
    58→
    59→  getLayout: (workspaceId: string) => WorkspaceLayoutState;
    60→  setLayout: (workspaceId: string, layout: Partial<WorkspaceLayoutState>) => void;
    61→
    62→  setActiveContentTab: (workspaceId: string, tab: ContentTab) => void;
    63→  setSelectedFilePath: (workspaceId: string, path: string | null) => void;
    64→  setSidebarCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) => void;
    65→  setChatPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) => void;
    66→  setContentPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) => void;
    67→  setPendingTerminalCommand: (workspaceId: string, command: string | null) => void;
    68→  setSimulatorUdid: (workspaceId: string, udid: string | null) => void;
    69→  setChatTabState: (
    70→    workspaceId: string,
    71→    sessionIds: string[],
    72→    activeSessionId: string | null
    73→  ) => void;
    74→  setTerminalTabState: (
    75→    workspaceId: string,
    76→    tabs: PersistedTerminalTab[],
    77→    activeTabId: string | null,
    78→    nextNum: number
    79→  ) => void;
    80→
    81→  clearWorkspaceLayout: (workspaceId: string) => void;
    82→  resetAll: () => void;
    83→}
    84→
    85→export const defaultLayout: WorkspaceLayoutState = {
    86→  activeContentTab: "changes",
    87→  selectedFilePath: null,
    88→  sidebarCollapsed: false,
    89→  browserTabs: [],
    90→  activeBrowserTabId: null,
    91→  chatPanelCollapsed: false,
    92→  contentPanelCollapsed: false,
    93→  fileTreePinned: true,
    94→  chatTabSessionIds: [],
    95→  activeChatTabSessionId: null,
    96→  pendingTerminalCommand: null,
    97→  simulatorUdid: null,
    98→  terminalTabs: [],
    99→  activeTerminalTabId: null,
   100→  nextTerminalNum: 1,
   101→};
   102→
   103→export const useWorkspaceLayoutStore = create<WorkspaceLayoutStore>()(
   104→  devtools(
   105→    persist(
   106→      (set, get) => ({
   107→        layouts: {},
   108→
   109→        getLayout: (workspaceId) => {
   110→          const { layouts } = get();
   111→          return { ...defaultLayout, ...(layouts[workspaceId] ?? {}) };
   112→        },
   113→
   114→        setLayout: (workspaceId, updates) =>
   115→          set(
   116→            (state) => ({
   117→              layouts: {
   118→                ...state.layouts,
   119→                [workspaceId]: {
   120→                  ...defaultLayout,
   121→                  ...state.layouts[workspaceId],
   122→                  ...updates,
   123→                },
   124→              },
   125→            }),
   126→            false,
   127→            "workspaceLayout/setLayout"
   128→          ),
   129→
   130→        setActiveContentTab: (workspaceId, tab) =>
   131→          set(
   132→            (state) => ({
   133→              layouts: {
   134→                ...state.layouts,
   135→                [workspaceId]: {
   136→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   137→                  activeContentTab: tab,
   138→                },
   139→              },
   140→            }),
   141→            false,
   142→            "workspaceLayout/setActiveContentTab"
   143→          ),
   144→
   145→        setSelectedFilePath: (workspaceId, path) =>
   146→          set(
   147→            (state) => ({
   148→              layouts: {
   149→                ...state.layouts,
   150→                [workspaceId]: {
   151→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   152→                  selectedFilePath: path,
   153→                },
   154→              },
   155→            }),
   156→            false,
   157→            "workspaceLayout/setSelectedFilePath"
   158→          ),
   159→
   160→        setSidebarCollapsed: (workspaceId, collapsed) =>
   161→          set(
   162→            (state) => ({
   163→              layouts: {
   164→                ...state.layouts,
   165→                [workspaceId]: {
   166→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   167→                  sidebarCollapsed: collapsed,
   168→                },
   169→              },
   170→            }),
   171→            false,
   172→            "workspaceLayout/setSidebarCollapsed"
   173→          ),
   174→
   175→        setChatPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId, collapsed) =>
   176→          set(
   177→            (state) => ({
   178→              layouts: {
   179→                ...state.layouts,
   180→                [workspaceId]: {
   181→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   182→                  chatPanelCollapsed: collapsed,
   183→                },
   184→              },
   185→            }),
   186→            false,
   187→            "workspaceLayout/setChatPanelCollapsed"
   188→          ),
   189→
   190→        setContentPanelCollapsed: (workspaceId, collapsed) =>
   191→          set(
   192→            (state) => ({
   193→              layouts: {
   194→                ...state.layouts,
   195→                [workspaceId]: {
   196→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   197→                  contentPanelCollapsed: collapsed,
   198→                },
   199→              },
   200→            }),
   201→            false,
   202→            "workspaceLayout/setContentPanelCollapsed"
   203→          ),
   204→
   205→        setChatTabState: (workspaceId, sessionIds, activeSessionId) =>
   206→          set(
   207→            (state) => ({
   208→              layouts: {
   209→                ...state.layouts,
   210→                [workspaceId]: {
   211→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   212→                  chatTabSessionIds: sessionIds,
   213→                  activeChatTabSessionId: activeSessionId,
   214→                },
   215→              },
   216→            }),
   217→            false,
   218→            "workspaceLayout/setChatTabState"
   219→          ),
   220→
   221→        setTerminalTabState: (workspaceId, tabs, activeTabId, nextNum) =>
   222→          set(
   223→            (state) => ({
   224→              layouts: {
   225→                ...state.layouts,
   226→                [workspaceId]: {
   227→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   228→                  terminalTabs: tabs,
   229→                  activeTerminalTabId: activeTabId,
   230→                  nextTerminalNum: nextNum,
   231→                },
   232→              },
   233→            }),
   234→            false,
   235→            "workspaceLayout/setTerminalTabState"
   236→          ),
   237→
   238→        setPendingTerminalCommand: (workspaceId, command) =>
   239→          set(
   240→            (state) => ({
   241→              layouts: {
   242→                ...state.layouts,
   243→                [workspaceId]: {
   244→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   245→                  pendingTerminalCommand: command,
   246→                },
   247→              },
   248→            }),
   249→            false,
   250→            "workspaceLayout/setPendingTerminalCommand"
   251→          ),
   252→
   253→        setSimulatorUdid: (workspaceId, udid) =>
   254→          set(
   255→            (state) => ({
   256→              layouts: {
   257→                ...state.layouts,
   258→                [workspaceId]: {
   259→                  ...(state.layouts[workspaceId] || defaultLayout),
   260→                  simulatorUdid: udid,
   261→                },
   262→              },
   263→            }),
   264→            false,
   265→            "workspaceLayout/setSimulatorUdid"
   266→          ),
   267→
   268→        clearWorkspaceLayout: (workspaceId) =>
   269→          set(
   270→            (state) => {
   271→              const { [workspaceId]: _removed, ...remaining } = state.layouts;
   272→              return { layouts: remaining };
   273→            },
   274→            false,
   275→            "workspaceLayout/clearWorkspaceLayout"
   276→          ),
   277→
   278→        resetAll: () => set({ layouts: {} }, false, "workspaceLayout/resetAll"),
   279→      }),
   280→      {
   281→        name: "workspace-layout-store",
   282→        version: 10,
   283→        partialize: (state) => ({
   284→          ...state,
   285→          layouts: Object.fromEntries(
   286→            Object.entries(state.layouts).map(([id, layout]) => [
   287→              id,
   288→              {
   289→                ...layout,
   290→                pendingTerminalCommand: null,
   291→                terminalTabs: [],
   292→                activeTerminalTabId: null,
   293→                nextTerminalNum: 1,
   294→              },
   295→            ])
   296→          ),
   297→        }),
   298→        // No migration — stale v9 data falls back to defaultLayout
   299→        migrate: () => ({ layouts: {} }) as unknown as WorkspaceLayoutStore,
   300→      }
   301→    ),
   302→    {
   303→      name: "workspace-layout-store",
   304→      enabled: import.meta.env.DEV,
   305→    }
   306→  )
   307→);
   308→
   309→/**
   310→ * Stable Actions - Call from anywhere without causing re-renders
   311→ */
   312→export const workspaceLayoutActions = {
   313→  getLayout: (workspaceId: string) => useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().getLayout(workspaceId),
   314→  setLayout: (workspaceId: string, layout: Partial<WorkspaceLayoutState>) =>
   315→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setLayout(workspaceId, layout),
   316→  setActiveContentTab: (workspaceId: string, tab: ContentTab) =>
   317→    useWorkspaceLayoutStore.getState().setActiveContentTab(workspaceId, tab),
   318→  setSelectedFilePath: (workspaceId: string, path: string | null) =>

   320→  setSidebarCollapsed: (workspaceId: string, collapsed: boolean) =>
